challenge.npc_text: Sure. Standard rules. Five minutes, no subs, no pausing, no noodling. No complaining about the controller either. I have a spare and it's worse.

choices.text: Let's go. I've been reading up.

lose.npc_text: Tactically, that was a masterclass. A masterclass in what not to do. You pressed too high, dribbled into the box on every single chance, and ran the same corner routine so many times it stopped being a mistake and became a philosophy. I have notes. Ten pages. Do you want the narrative?

choices.text: I need to think about this.

pre_win.npc_text: Wait. You're — what is happening. You're holding the line. Pressing at 70. You didn't panic. This is structured. Who are you.

choices.text: I've been thinking about the game differently.

win_book.npc_text: 'Winning Ugly'? You read 'Winning Ugly'? That's — I mean — that's actually a legitimate psychological framework. Chapter nine is almost offensively correct. Fine. You won. The better tactician won today. I need to sit with this for a while.

win_vague.npc_text: You actually won. I don't know what just happened but it was structured. You had a plan and executed it. Against me. Against my record. I respect that more than I'm comfortable admitting.

post_win.npc_text: Look. You won. Fair and square, even if I don't fully understand how. We're stepping out to process this. Lab's yours. Don't touch the compile terminal. And whatever you do — don't touch the specimen cup. I'm serious. I don't know exactly what's in it anymore.
